programme.desc: Jurors learn about forensic linguistics on the stand; authorities claim Melanie wrote the letters.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: FBI specialist, James Fitzgerald, analyzes two anonymous letters sent during the investigation.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Forensic Examination Of The Evidence Collected
programme.desc: Jurors learn about the evidence that prosecutors believed linked Melanie McGuire to the murder.

programme.title: Suitcase Killer Murder Trial
programme.desc: The forensic scientist who examined the plastic bags containing Bill McGuire's remains testifies.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: Opening statements began with the story of how child abuse at home gradually led to a family homicide. Six-year-old Corey Micciolo lost his life by blunt force trauma suffered when his father, Christopher Gregor, forced him to run on a treadmill because he was too fat. The defendant was charged with first-degree murder and endangering the welfare of a child. He faced a maximum Penalty of 40 years in prison.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: Prosecutors began their evidence to the jury with testimony from Corey Micciolo's mother. She confirmed that the defendant, Christopher Gregor, entered Corey's life when he was 5 years old. Jurors learned of the family drama Corey was born in. Breanna Micciolo was only 17 years old and in the 11th grade dealing with a methamphetamine addiction when Corey was born. His father, Gregor, didn't even know he was the father until he was 5 years old.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Jury learned about the emotions of a mother who was in fear of her young child's life.

programme.title: Treadmill Abuse Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Infamous video of the treadmill abuse of young Corey Micciolo was shown in full to the jury. His father, Christopher Gregor, told detectives that he pushed his son hard in the Fitness Center because he was concerned about his weight. Apparently, Corey was getting too fat. Next, what life was like in the last weeks of this six-year-old's life was relayed back to jurors. To do this--prosecutors introduced text messages between Corey's parents, Corey's bruises, and hospital visits.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: Law enforcement showed up at Alex Woodworth's house to find clues to help their investigation into what led to his death. At the time, Alex was a barista at a coffee shop, but he aspired to be a philosophy professor.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: The officer who found the murder weapon took the stand in the killer girlfriend murder trial. He searched the snowy, muddy area near the crime scene where Alex Woodworth's body was found. He spotted the knife near a farm house.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: Prosecutors hoped by tracking the movements of Ezra McCandless on the day she killed Alex Woodworth, it would give jurors a sense of what was on her mind. Police put together a crime scene reconstruction of where Ezra fatally stabbed Alex.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.desc: Investigators collected dozens of items to help figure out what happened when Ezra McCandless killed Alex Woodworth. She claimed from the beginning she was a victim, defending her life.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Police Investigators And What They Found
programme.desc: The State continued to call upon police investigators to explain to jurors what they examined at the crime scene where Alex Woodworth was killed. They scoured the entire dairy farm area processing key items and finding important clues.

programme.desc: 14 years after an attack of a jogger, police trace a family tree using genetic genealogy and find the rapist.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: DNA science finally identifies both killer and victim from a murder that took place back in Florida in 1991.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Can the groundbreaking tool of investigative genetic genealogy solve the tragic 1993 murder of Melissa Lee, which leaves detectives baffled in Bothell, Washington?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1986, police struggle to find a young mother's killer. Years later, can genetic genealogy provide answers?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 2010, Sherry Black is found stabbed to death in her bookstore in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police fail to identify her killer until advancements in DNA science reignite the case.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1985, a young woman is awoken by an intruder who sexually assaults her at gunpoint in her own condo. The determination of the survivor will lead her on a decades-long journey to justice through a revolutionary forensic science technique.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The murder of Carol Sue Klaber is cold for decades until new technology can finally bring her killer to light.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1990 Mississippi, a woman is kidnapped and raped at knifepoint. Police are unable to identify the perpetrator. It'll take forward-thinking investigators, state-of-the-art science and brave survivors to put this man behind bars.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A woman is shocked to learn her DNA helped detectives identify a Jane Doe that remained a mystery for decades.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young woman went missing from her home on a bright spring morning. After several days of searching, agents suspected a paroled criminal who frequented her health club may have abducted her.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1980, a maid discovered that a copy machine, placed in a busy Las Vegas casino, was actually a bomb.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A drug dealer engaged in a shootout with Nebraska state troopers and evaded capture after a high-speed chase.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1982, a California highway patrol officer pulled a young woman over for speeding. He bullied her into sex and then killed her. He then used a decade of police experience to try to cover up his crime and convince investigators of his innocence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, two men used a sledgehammer to break into a Camden, New Jersey home. Police tracked the suspects to a nearby park but the criminals were able to escape after a nearly fatal shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI began an undercover operation to expose the Chicago Mafia's infiltration into the city's surrounding suburbs. The investigation resulted in the arrests of former police chief Michael J. Corbitt and Alan Masters for murder and racketeering.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the FBI was overwhelmed with investigations in Seattle which had the third-highest number of bank robberies in the nation. Agents focused on a series of heists in which two heavily armed men seemed to know where the banks stored their money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, the son of a successful real estate developer was kidnapped in Miami. After the father received a $3 million ransom demand from a self-proclaimed terrorist, the FBI became involved and agents began to suspect a pair of Colombian nationals.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Angered by the government's policy in the Middle East, Mir Qazi brutally gunned down two members of the CIA as they waited in their cars at a stoplight. The killer fled the country and was placed on the FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When authorities in a small Washington town began investigating the disappearance of a young woman, they had no idea it would turn into a nationwide hunt for a sadistic sexual predator. The FBI was called in to help track down Darren Dee O'Neall.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A pair of convicted felons escaped the custody of a local deputy and fled across the country in a wave of abductions and car thefts. When a foreign military officer disappeared along the trail, the FBI believed the pair were growing more violent.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A bank vault was blasted into from the ceiling, and over $2 million in valuables was missing. Agents were able to track down one of the robbers' friends and attempted to use him as bait to reel in the crooks.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young woman went missing from her home on a bright spring morning. After several days of searching, agents suspected a paroled criminal who frequented her health club may have abducted her.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: No suspect had emerged after the rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984. The killer was a ruthless and terrifying predator, the newest member of an infamous group known as "serial killers."

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990s Philadelphia, a ruthless grab for power led to a full-scale mob war. Using informants and high-tech surveillance, the FBI infiltrated the most powerful mob family in the city and sought to bring down the organization.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On July 4, 1987, seven inmates broke out of a high-security prison in New Mexico. The daring escape was orchestrated by convicted murderer William Wayne Gilbert, who was serving a life sentence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In February 1988, a record store clerk in Ohio was fatally shot in his van. After discovering thousands of dollars in the vehicle, police knew that robbery was not the motive.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.desc: Musician and special needs teacher Jane Longhurst vanished during her day off work after making a plan to go swimming with her friend's boyfriend. Five weeks later, Jane's partially decomposed body was found burning 25 miles from Jane's home.

programme.desc: Convicted of shooting a man and 21 other felonies, Miti Maugaotega Jr. receives one of the longest prison sentences in Hawaiʻi history. Behind bars, he joins a prison gang and becomes a tornado of death and destruction.

programme.desc: In 2006, Darlene VanderGiesen received threatening emails and then disappeared. Tracking the source of the emails led police to the home of Daphne Wright, where they believe a murder was committed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After shooting his victims in the head, the killer staged the scene, placed the incriminating evidence into a plastic bag and tossed it into the river.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How did the stalker obtain the security system code for his victim's home? How did he steal her personal photographs? Police needed answers and they found them in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother claims that an intruder murdered her two sons, but lacking a motive, police use blood-spatter analysis and behavioral profiling to reconstruct a different scenario.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A driver said he couldn't have hit and killed a pedestrian because his Jeep had been sold months ago.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the case of the Center City Rapist and the murder of Shannon Schieber, Philadelphia authorities use an anonymous letter and geographic profiling to hone in on a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One warm summer afternoon, the town of Verona, Wisconsin faced its first triple homicide. To solve the case, investigators had to delve into the world of high rollers and offshore betting.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A wealthy man and his wife were attacked by three masked men outside their luxurious Louisiana home. He was shot dead and she was forced to open their hidden safe.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A child was killed in a hit-and-run. The prosecutor has an accident reconstruction expert come to trial.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman is stabbed more than 100 times. The killer leaves DNA behind, but investigators must play a cat-and-mouse game to obtain a suspect's DNA to match.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2001, Ginger Hayes and her infant son Nicholas were abducted during a carjacking and the crime had been reported by a witness within minutes of occurrence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An infant was rushed to the emergency room with serious breathing problems. Within months, there were more than 30 cases. Doctors searched frantically for the cause and a cure.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-year-old boy was rushed to the hospital suffering from brain seizures and breathing difficulties.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder investigation in Florida crosses jurisdictions from New York and Jamaica. The police rely on cell phone mapping, wiretapping and a host of forensic evidence to link a suspect to a murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a dedicated, well-respected teacher disappeared, police had to determine if she'd gone on vacation without telling anyone, or if she was the victim of foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer used poison which had no taste.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In little more than a month, two women who lived in the same apartment complex were brutally murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Time of death becomes pivotal after a pregnant woman is found murdered in her air-conditioned bedroom.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A girl claimed she had been abducted. She recounted what happened but things didn't add up to police.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young TV news producer is raped and murdered in her apartment. Investigators turn to the Commonwealth of Virginia's DNA Databank.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a serial killer was terrorizing the city of St. Louis, the FBI didn't need lab tests, fingerprints or any of the usual methods. All they needed was a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2003, St. Cloud teenager Jason MacLennan returned home after a night out with friends and discovered his father Ken's body lying in a pool of blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott, police use a garbage bag to connect a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1987 disappearance of Houston resident Tracy Jo Shine is recalled. The investigation went cold until 2000 when a "cold case squad" learned that the woman's ex-boyfriend Michael Neal had bragged about killing her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Rosemary Anderson is found dead on the side of the road, her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man - serial killer, Eric Edgar Cooke.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman went missing, friends and family were determined to find her. Their worst fears were confirmed weeks later when her body was discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspicious fire swept through an apartment killing two young women. A closer look at the fire scene revealed something hidden in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Who was the sexual offender who murdered two boys in Nebraska? The key to stopping him would be the unique composition of the "junk rope" he used to tie his victims.
